items.headline: New residential development approved on Sherly Place
items.summary: Five new single-family homes permitted in the Sherly Place development (lots 1-5 on Sherly Pl and Lincoln Ave), totaling nearly $2.5 million in construction value. This represents meaningful infill housing development in an established San José neighborhood.

items.headline: City Council Reviews FY 2026-27 Capital Improvement Program
items.summary: The council held a study session on April 14 to examine Mountain View's planned capital projects for the 2026-27 fiscal year. This preview of upcoming infrastructure and development investments gives residents early visibility into what physical changes and improvements the city is prioritizing.

items.headline: New tenant protections ordinance introduced for Sunnyvale
items.summary: The City Council introduced an ordinance amending tenant protections in Chapter 19.71 of the municipal code. Residents should monitor this measure as it could affect renter rights and landlord-tenant relationships in Sunnyvale.

items.headline: City reports on youth services, library facilities, code enforcement
items.summary: The City Council received status updates on its Children and Youth Services Master Plan, youth programs including the Safe Summer Initiative, and plans for library facility improvements and customer experience. These reports provide insight into how San José is investing in youth services and public library infrastructure.

items.headline: Downtown building seeks historic status upgrade and 5,000 sq ft bonus
items.summary: A property at 405 Kipling Street is requesting elevation from Category 4 to Category 2 historic resource status, which would allow an additional 5,000 square feet of floor area as an incentive for historic and seismic rehabilitation. This could significantly increase the building's development potential in downtown Palo Alto.
